title: Pluchea purpurascens
intro: Pluchea purpurascens, also known as the Purple Pluchea, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The Purple Pluchea has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Purple Pluchea is often prepared as an infusion or decoction to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the form of tinctures and essential oils.
taxonomy: Pluchea purpurascens is classified as a flowering plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of Pluchea, a genus of flowering plants. The genus Pluchea is part of the tribe Inuleae, which is a group of plants within the larger Asteraceae family. Within the tribe Inuleae, Pluchea purpurascens is one of the species found in the region of South Africa. The classification of Pluchea purpurascens reflects its botanical relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.
